name: Planetary Inc.
short_description: A short incremental game about building a career by destroying planets, meeting corporate goals, and investing in profitable — and maybe not so ethical — ventures.
detailed_description:
A short incremental clicker game about building a career by destroying planets, hitting corporate goals, and investing in profitable — and maybe not-so-ethical — activities.

Click on planets until they are completely destroyed and get paid for it.

Use your money to buy upgrades that speed up the process and automate your work.

Make even more money by buying shares in companies that dominate the galaxy. Earn passive income or go all in and take over the market.

Purchase upgrades to boost your profits and become more efficient in the space cleanup business. Don’t worry — no species will be harmed. 100% legitimate. Trust us.

Hit every goal, take over as the new boss, and dominate the interplanetary market.

Explore the prestige tree.

Reset your progress to unlock permanent upgrades and progress faster with every run.

reviews.review_text: Just got this, and enjoyed it for the few minutes up until the first prestige. Once I prestiged, I noticed my investments stopped working, they are no longer running. I tried relogging, but it did not resolve the issue.  The money I get per planet breaks display and what I was making was also off. It was showing breaking the planet giving 400$, but I would only get like 80. At the moment, I cant recommend it, as it appears to be fundamentally broken. If the dev patches this, I can see myself really liking this for a work a game.

This game is a bang for the buck! You play as an employee of a clearly shady company, tasked with destroying planets, while absolutely destroying your left mouse button in the process to earn money.

Like any good clicker, there are plenty of upgrades to buy (including my beloved auto-clicks) and milestones to complete for extra cash. You can even make investments for passive income.

Everything revolves around the prestige system, which you unlock as you progress. It lets you restart your run with permanent upgrades, giving the game a very addictive aspect.

There’s also some intriguing lore hidden beneath it all :)

If you’re into games that slowly consume your life, this one’s definitely for you.
